in the getting of clients, the attraction of clients, and we were right after molehills and the mountains and so if you flip over to your page 55, that's approximately where we were. And we're going to go all the way through and finish getting clients, we're going to talk about getting paid, financial compensation structures, and then we're going to go through all the rest of the documents and the samples and so forth of things that I have to show you. So if you're on page 55, Keith Oberman on MSIBC, the countdown show, starts every night with the 10 stories that they're going to talk about coming up, click, click, click, click, click, click, click, and the voiceover is which of these stories will you be talking about tomorrow? It's a very good question, you know, which of, which part of you are people going to be talking about tomorrow? Because if you are not being talked about continuously, good or bad by the way, better if it's more good than bad, but if you are not being talked about continuously by your clientele amongst themselves and to others, right? Now, for example, again, we watched the Muse last night and so think back to the beginning of that segment where, what's that actor's name, is telling, is telling the comedian, Jeff, yeah, very good, I'm very underrated actor. So Jeff Bridges is now telling the other guy about her, right? Well, that's exactly the way you would want somebody telling somebody about you. That won't happen by accident, you know, that has to sort of be planted, but if she's got like 10 people telling that story to somebody that week, she has no marketing problems, right? She has no client getting problems, but you got to be continuously talked about amongst your clientele, right? You got to be the topic of interest and speculation and curiosity and blabbering and you got to be talked about in your market. There are, I never go to them. Occasionally somebody will download something and send it to me, but there are like chat rooms where people go and talk about Dan Kennedy, okay? On one level it's sort of sad and pathetic, you know, it's sort of in the get a life category, you know? But for example, Paris Hilton, who of course makes all her money by being Paris Hilton, okay? And no one should miss the lesson of that. The current going rate, for example, to have Paris Hilton at your party for two hours is $125,000, okay? She is sometimes doing two a night. See now where I was going to go is you can get in and a call for less money now that she's dead, but see both your remark and mine would be better off not said. But anyway, think about this. She is getting paid large sums of money for who she is. There are at last count over 300 different websites where people go to talk about Paris Hilton. Some of them are very negative and critical and nasty, you know, the sites about the 10 worst celebrities in Hollywood and their behavior this week. Others are fan club type sites and everything in between. But her elevation to a form of stardom and certainly to being able to make a lot of money by being Paris Hilton is all about being talked about. The lunatic kid, what's her name? Brittany. In case you didn't notice, she's been in the news for the last three days nonstop because she did her one day in rehab, which is an all-time record, but it suggests a business, you know, rehab in 24 hours or less. We bring you in, we fix you, we get your ass out because you got to go make a movie. And I bet you could charge more for that than six months worth of rehab and you'd get them in and out in the day. You only got to feed them three times. But she has shaved her head and gotten tattoos all over her head. Now I don't know about the long-term strategy of all that, but she's the only thing being talked about on CNN. I mean, when I clicked on CNN last night, a head of the story of the North Korean nuke negotiations, which might actually like affect us. I mean, look, I remember being under the wood desk, you know, doing the drill, which you know, Lewis Black's routine about that is great, you know. That's the first time you realize that all adults are morons when you're a kid, right, is when they are putting you under a wood desk to protect you from a large flaming orb object that is going to descend upon the building.
